sanskrit:
अव्यक्तादीनि भूतानि व्यक्तमध्यानि भारत।
अव्यक्तनिधनान्येव तत्र का परिदेवना॥
transliteration:
avyaktādīni bhūtāni vyakta-madhyāni bhārata |
avyakta-nidhanāny eva tatra kā paridevanā ||
literal_meaning: Beings are unmanifest at first, manifest in the middle, and unmanifest again at the end; so why lament?
contextual_meaning: Krishna reframes impermanence as a natural cycle, not a personal loss, to dissolve Arjuna’s anticipatory grief.
psychological_state: Arjuna is stuck in future-oriented rumination, catastrophizing the death of loved ones; Krishna offers a mindfulness-based reframe that normalizes the life-death-life arc.
trigger_scenario: A parent receiving a terminal diagnosis for their child and spiraling into ‘why my baby?’—until a therapist walks them through the universality of birth-life-death as a shared human story rather than a personal punishment.
anchor_text: What appears and disappears is only form; the essence cycles on—grieve not.
